title: Multnomah County Summer & Heat Related Resources - 211info
text: UPDATED: May 1, 2026, at 6:11 PM Cooling centers are not open at this time. Multnomah County and the City of Portland will continue to monitor weather conditions via the National Weather Service and send future alerts as necessary. Year-round resources remain available. Help for When It’s Hot – Multnomah County Cooling Resources When Does Multnomah County Open Cooling Centers or Shelters? Cool Spaces Near You Interactive map of cool spaces , including: library branches, pools, community centers, splash pads, misting stations, and fountains. Sign Up for Alerts Sign up for text alerts from 211info when weather alerts are called in Multnomah County, TEXT the keyword “pdxshelter” to 898211 (TXT211) Sign up to receive email alerts from 211info when weather alerts are called in Multnomah County Year-Round Shelters & Daytime Drop-In Centers Locate year-round shelters and daytime drop-in centers
